name: Snakemake workflow for a typical DFT calculation
description: Parameterised Snakemake workflow encoding the canonical DFT calculation pipeline: structure preparation → SCF convergence → property extraction (DOS / band structure / response) → post-processing → archival. The workflow is reproducible (DAG cached, intermediate files versioned) and portable across local machines, SLURM clusters, and Singularity containers. v1 targets Siesta but the rule structure is code-agnostic; CP2K / Quantum ESPRESSO / VASP variants drop in by swapping the rule scripts.

parameters.label: Input structure (CIF / POSCAR / FDF)
parameters.note: Relative path within the workflow root. Snakemake's input directive depends on this; a missing file fails the DAG immediately.

parameters.label: DFT engine
parameters.note: One of: siesta, cp2k, quantum-espresso, vasp. Determines which rule scripts the workflow dispatches to.

parameters.label: k-point mesh (Monkhorst-Pack)
parameters.note: Comma-free string parsed into three integers. Workflow generates a k-grid convergence sweep when sweepKpoints is true.

parameters.label: Energy cutoff (Ry)
parameters.note: Plane-wave or real-space mesh cutoff. Validated against pseudopotential recommendations in the rule.

parameters.label: Smearing scheme
parameters.note: fermi-dirac, methfessel-paxton (order N), gaussian, cold-smearing. Choice affects k-point convergence rate.

parameters.label: Smearing width (eV)
parameters.note: Validated against the system's band gap (insulator: smaller; metal: larger but ≤0.1 eV).

parameters.label: Output directory
parameters.note: Snakemake materialises rule outputs here; versioned with workflow git hash by default.

parameters.label: Run k-point convergence sweep
parameters.note: If true, the workflow runs the calculation across k-meshes 4×4×4 → 16×16×16 and emits a convergence-vs-k report.

parameters.label: Container image (Singularity)
parameters.note: Optional path to a .sif image bundling the DFT code + analysis tooling. When set, every rule runs inside the container.

validation: structureFile must exist before workflow start (Snakemake input check)
dftCode must be one of {siesta, cp2k, quantum-espresso, vasp}
kPointMesh must parse to three positive integers
energyCutoff_Ry must be > 0; recommended ≥ 200 Ry for Siesta DZP basis sets
smearing must be a known scheme; methfessel-paxton requires order ≥ 1
smearingWidth_eV must be > 0 and < band gap for insulators
outputDir must not pre-exist or must be empty (avoid clobbering previous runs)
every Snakemake rule must declare both input and output explicitly so the DAG is reproducible
container path, if set, must point to a readable .sif file
references: Köster & Rahmann (2012) — Snakemake: a scalable bioinformatics workflow engine, Bioinformatics 28(19)
Pizzi et al. (2016) — AiiDA: automated interactive infrastructure and database for computational science, Comp. Mat. Sci. 111 (alternative workflow engine; cross-reference)
Sustainable Materials Modelling (https://nomad-lab.eu) — open data deposition expected at workflow end
